title: Police raid LGBT+ Black Cat Tavern in Los Angeles
description: Undercover police raided LGBT+ bar the Black Cat Tavern in Los Angeles after patrons dared to kiss to celebrate the new year, arresting 14 and charging 6 with lewd conduct, and leading to a protest outside on Feb 11.

title: Second Palmer Raids begin against US radicals
description: The start of the second Palmer Raids, an attempt by the US Department of Justice to arrest and deport radical workers and leftists, especially anarchists. Some 6000 were arrested and held without trial and over 500 deported.

title: US authorities arrest striking Guam teachers
description: 100s of teachers in Guam walked out on strike demanding a 30% cost of living pay increase. US colonial authorities fired most strikers and arrested dozens, and after 58 days, the strike was defeated.

title: Black Panthers Bunchy Carter and John Huggins murdered
description: Two Black Panthers, Bunchy Carter and John Huggins, were murdered by Black nationalist group US. The US Organization was being used by the FBI as part of their COINTELPRO programme to neutralise Black radical groups.

title: BLM protester Maile Hampton arrested for 'felony lynching'
description: Black Lives Matter protester Maile Hampton was arrested in Sacramento, CA for "felony lynching" for trying to prevent a fellow demonstrator being arrested. While the law was renamed after some outrage it continues to be used against BLM.

title: Angela Davis born in Birmingham, Alabama
description: Angela Davis was born in Birmingham, AL. Revolutionary, civil rights organiser and third woman to make the FBI's most wanted list, Davis was also associated with the Black Panthers.

title: Porvenir massacre by US troops and Texas Rangers
description: A group of US ranchers, Texas Rangers, and United States Army cavalry soldiers went into the Mexican-American village of Porvenir and murdered 15 men and boys, the youngest of whom was only 16, including Longino Flores.

title: Fort Leavenworth military prisoners go on strike
description: Inmates at Fort Leavenworth military prison went on strike. They succeeded in obtaining early release of some prisoners including conscientious objectors, and setting up a grievance committee to oversee improvements.

title: Greensboro lunch counter sit-in begins
description: Today is the start of Black History Month in the US. It is also the anniversary of 1 Feb 1960 in Greensboro, NC, when four Black college students refused to move from a Woolworth's lunch counter after they were denied service.

title: US anarchist Michele Schirru arrested in Rome
description: US anarchist Michael Schirru was arrested in his hotel room in Rome for coming to Italy with the intention to kill its fascist dictator Benito Mussolini. He was imprisoned and later executed by firing squad.

title: Tom Mooney convicted on framed bombing charges
description: US labour leader Tom Mooney, who was framed for a bombing, was convicted and sentenced to death. Mass campaigning eventually forced President Wilson to intervene to grant him a stay of execution, then commutation.

title: Orangeburg massacre of Black students in South Carolina
description: The Orangeburg massacre took place in South Carolina when police opened fire on Black SC State students, killing three and wounding 27 after Black student-led protests at a segregated bowling alley.

title: Emma Goldman arrested under Comstock Act
description: Lithuanian-born Jewish anarchist Emma Goldman was arrested in New York City on charges of violating the Comstock Act, for distributing information about birth control.

title: Black WWII vet Isaac Woodard blinded by police
description: Isaac Woodard, a 26-year-old Black WWII veteran, still in uniform from being honorably discharged that day, was taken to jail, beaten, and permanently blinded by police in South Carolina.

title: Malcolm X assassinated in New York
description: Malcolm X, instrumental speaker and activist of the American civil rights and Black power movements, was assassinated while preparing to address a crowd of supporters in New York.

title: Wiyot massacre of Indigenous people in California
description: The Wiyot massacre took place when white settlers murdered up to 250 Indigenous Wiyot people at Tuluwat, California, then expelled them from their land. But they and their descendants kept fighting and by 2019 got back most of their land.

title: Native activists begin Wounded Knee occupation
description: Armed Native American activists occupied Wounded Knee, South Dakota, in protest at corruption and US treaty breaches with Native Americans. Despite state violence and killings they held out for 71 days and galvanised huge support.

title: Birth control activist Ida Rauh dies
description: Jewish actor, sculptor, suffragette and socialist Ida Rauh died in NYC, aged 92. She had been arrested and convicted in 1916 for passing out birth control pamphlets, and was a union organiser with the Women's Trade Union League.

title: Rodney King beaten by LAPD officers
description: Rodney King was brutally beaten by police in Los Angeles. Despite it being caught on film (almost unheard of in those days), the officers were acquitted, and the city went up in flames in what were the biggest riots since 1968.

title: Anarchist Lucy Parsons dies in Chicago
description: Lucy Parsons, African-American anarchist, founding member of the Industrial Workers of the World (IWW), and powerful orator for women, working people, and political prisoners died in a house fire.

title: Gnadenhutten massacre by US revolutionary forces
description: The Gnadenhutten massacre took place in Ohio, when US forces in the revolutionary war massacred 96 Christian Lenape native Americans, scalping 39 children, 29 women and 28 men, before pillaging and burning their village.

title: 1935 Harlem Uprising against police brutality
description: The 1935 Harlem Uprising took place in NYC when Black people rebelled against police after rumours of brutality. Police banned a demo, and protests escalated, causing $200 million in damages, while three Black people were killed.

title: Ponce massacre during Puerto Rican independence protest
description: Police massacred 19 people at a peaceful demonstration and wounded hundreds in Puerto Rico. They were protesting for the release of imprisoned independence leader, Albizu Campos.

title: Police arrest 14 IWW members in Sioux City
description: Police in Sioux City arrested 14 members of the IWW for holding street meetings to stop the union growing. IWW members came to the town to be arrested and filled the jails until eventually the police caved and allowed meetings.

title: Arkansas bans anarchism and communism
description: The state of Arkansas joined most US states in banning anarchism and communism. The law to 'define and punish anarchy and Bolshevism' barred revolutionary activism and red and black flags, with up to six months jail as a punishment.

title: 101 IWW members tried under Espionage Act
description: 101 Wobblies (members of the IWW union) were put on trial in Chicago. They were charged with violating the espionage act for the opposition to World War I.

title: NYC Panther 21 arrested in coordinated raids
description: Several Black Panthers were arrested in raids across NYC. 21 were charged with conspiracy to murder police and bomb shops and trains based on false testimony from undercover agents. After NY's longest trial all were acquitted.

title: Martin Luther King Jr assassinated in Memphis
description: Martin Luther King Jr, legendary civil rights activist and non-violence advocate, was assassinated in Memphis, sparking riots across the US. He was in the city supporting a strike of garbage workers.

title: Black Panther Bobby Hutton murdered by Oakland police
description: 17-year-old Black Panther, Bobby Hutton, was murdered by police in Oakland, California. The first recruit to the Party, he was unarmed, shirtless and had surrendered to police when they shot him at least 10 times.

title: IWW supporter and abortion provider Marie Equi born
description: Radical doctor, feminist, anti-war activist, lesbian and IWW supporter Marie Diana Equi was born in New Bedford, Massachusetts. Later jailed for 3 years in San Quentin for opposing World War I.

title: Lucasville prison rebellion in Ohio begins
description: Possibly the longest prison rebellion in US history took place in Lucasville, Ohio. Black and white prisoners, including those in the Aryan Brotherhood, fought together against mistreatment by prison authorities.

title: Braintree robbery used to frame Sacco and Vanzetti
description: The Braintree robbery took place in Massachusetts. Italian anarchists Nicola Sacco and Bartolomeo Vanzetti were tried and executed on circumstantial evidence in what is widely considered a miscarriage of justice.

title: Ludlow massacre of striking miners in Colorado
description: US troops opened fire with machine guns and set fire to tents in a camp of striking miners and their families in Ludlow, Colorado. They burned two women, 11 children and shot 13 others. None were prosecuted.

title: Anaconda Road massacre of striking Butte miners
description: The Anaconda Road massacre took place in Butte, Montana, when mine guards employed by the Anaconda company shot 17 striking miners, killing one. All were shot in the back as they tried to flee. No one was charged for the killing.

title: Howard students stage segregated DC restaurant sit-in
description: 200 Black people, mostly students, held a sit-in at a segregated restaurant in Washington DC. The disruption forced HQ to order the branch to start serving Black customers. A key organiser was LGBT+ Pauli Murray.

title: California enacts first state forced sterilization law
description: The first state sterilisation law in California was enacted, remaining largely unopposed for nearly 70 years. It targeted patients in state hospitals and institutions for the mentally ill, as well as prison inmates.

title: US white supremacists arrested over Dominica invasion plot
description: 9 US and Canadian white supremacists were arrested as they prepared to invade Dominica. The racists, including founder of neo-Nazi website Stormfront, planned to establish a white dictatorship in the 99% non-white nation.

title: Native man Tacho lynched by white mob in Banning, CO
description: A Native American man named Tacho was lynched by a white mob in Banning, CO, after being accused of stealing horses. The media described him as a 'desperado of the worst type' in a racist report justifying the murder.

title: US general strike for the 8-hour day begins
description: 300-500k workers went on strike and rallies were held across the US, with the cry: 'Eight-hour day with no cut in pay.' The strike led to the Chicago Haymarket Affair and the start of May Day itself.

title: Armed Black Panthers march into California Capitol
description: Armed Black Panthers marched into the California Capitol protesting a gun control bill which was targeting them. Supported by the NRA, Reagan signed the bill to stop Panther police self defence patrols.

title: Italian anarchist Andrea Salsedo dies in BoI custody
description: Italian anarchist Andrea Salsedo died in New York City. Some sources claim he killed himself by jumping from a window of the Bureau of Investigation during interrogation; others claim he was murdered by investigators.

title: Kent State massacre by Ohio National Guard
description: The Kent State massacre took place when Ohio National Guard fired 67 rounds into a crowd of students protesting against the bombing of Cambodia, killing four and wounding nine others including bystanders.

title: Hard Hat Riot attacks NYC anti-war protesters
description: The Hard Hat Riot took place in NYC after 200 construction workers attacked 1000 mostly young people protesting the Vietnam War in an operation organised by the New York state in conjunction with the White House.

title: Goldman and Berkman found No Conscription League
description: Alexander Berkman and Emma Goldman founded the No Conscription League in the US to discourage young men from registering for the draft during World War I. Both later fined $10,000 and jailed for 2 years.

title: Pullman railroad strike begins in Chicago
description: The Pullman railroad strike began in Chicago after the firing of 3 workers. The biggest strike in US history to date, it was only eventually broken by federal government troops and the killing of at least 24 strikers.

title: Police kill two students at Jackson State College
description: Police opened fire on a small group of Black students protesting at Jackson State College, Mississippi, killing two, including a bystander, and wounding 12.

title: US government seizes the railroads to break strike
description: The US government seized the railroads to stop a nationwide strike. Workers walked out anyway and only the President's threat to draft strikers and call the army to works as scabs forced them back.

title: IWW founder Big Bill Haywood dies in Moscow
description: 'Big Bill' Haywood, former mineworker and founding member of the Industrial Workers of the World revolutionary union, died in Moscow.

title: Matewan WV shootout between miners and Pinkertons
description: A shootout took place in the town of Matewan, WV between striking miners and private detectives, who had arrived in town to evict miners' families. Seven detectives, one bystander and two miners were killed.

title: Black Panther Alex Rackley murdered amid COINTELPRO badjacketing
description: 19-year-old Black Panther Alex Rackley was murdered in New Haven, CT by fellow activists after wrongly being accused of being a police informant, amidst a campaign of infiltration and badjacketing by the FBI and police.

title: Eugene Debs imprisoned for Pullman strike role
description: Rail union leader Eugene Debs was imprisoned for 6 months for his role in the Pullman strike. The repression backfired on the state somewhat as Debs emerged a militant socialist.

title: Largest mass arrest in Rochester NY history
description: The largest mass arrest in the history of Rochester, New York, took place. Police rounded up 208 striking workers and even arrested one of the strikers' dogs. The strike spread and was won the following week.

title: IWW organiser Judi Bari's car bombed in Oakland
description: The car of revolutionary IWW construction worker and environmentalist Judi Bari was bombed, severely injuring her and wounding Darryl Cherney. They had been campaigning to protect ancient redwood forests in California from logging.

title: Memorial Day Massacre of Chicago steelworkers
description: The Memorial Day massacre occurred when Chicago police opened fire on a peaceful march of striking steel workers and their families. The police murdered 10, shot, beat and hospitalised many others. No police were prosecuted.

title: Tulsa race massacre destroys Black Wall Street
description: The Tulsa racist massacre took place leaving 300 dead and 10k homeless after a fake allegation of a Black boy attacking a white woman was used to stir up white supremacist violence. Racists destroyed a whole area dubbed Black Wall St.

title: Zoot Suit Riots begin in Los Angeles
description: 200 white US Navy sailors attacked zoot suit wearing Mexican-American children in LA. Police arrested the victims of the attacks and the city council even tried to ban the wearing of the suit. Zoot suit riots broke out in several cities.

title: Goldman and Berkman speak at No Conscription rally
description: Emma Goldman and Alexander Berkman were among the speakers at a meeting of the No Conscription League at the Hunts Point Palace in the Bronx. The League were organising opposition to US involvement in World War I.

title: Freedom Riders arrested in Jackson, Mississippi
description: A group of Freedom Riders including Kwame Ture, Gwendolyn Greene and Joan Mulholland were arrested in Jackson, Mississippi, for protesting against the unlawful segregation of public transport.

title: Fannie Lou Hamer arrested and beaten in Mississippi
description: Civil rights activist Fannie Lou Hamer was arrested in Mississippi on trumped up charges and brutally beaten in jail on the orders of police. She had previously been sterilised without consent, as were many other Black women.

title: Anarchist Bartolomeo Vanzetti born in Italy
description: Italian anarchist Bartolomeo Vanzetti was born. Framed and executed alongside Nicola Sacco, their trial became a world-famous miscarriage of justice.

title: Division Street riots erupt in Chicago
description: Following the Puerto Rican Day Parade, a youth was shot in the leg by police, causing two days of riots in Chicago known as the Division Street riots.

title: Albuquerque riot after police shoot Latino youth
description: Rioting broke out in Albuquerque, New Mexico after police attempted to arrest a Latino youth and opened fire into a crowd, wounding several.

title: Black trans sex worker Mary Jones tried in NYC
description: Black trans US sex worker and army veteran Mary Jones was put on trial for pickpocketing a client who solicited her for sex. Found guilty, she was sentenced to 5 years in prison.

title: Police raid Eve's Hangout lesbian cafe in NYC
description: Eve's Hangout, a New York radical lesbian cafe run by Eve Adams, was raided by police, who destroyed texts. Adams was convicted of obscenity and deported. Being Jewish, she was later murdered in Auschwitz.

title: Police massacre Youngstown steelworkers' families
description: Police massacred 16 people and injured nearly 300 when striking steelworkers' wives and children demonstrated in their support in Youngstown, Ohio. Many were shot in the back as they ran from the police.

title: Chaney, Goodman and Schwerner murdered in Mississippi
description: Three American civil rights activists, James Earl Chaney, Andrew Goodman, and Michael 'Mickey' Schwerner, were murdered by police and KKK members in Mississippi.

title: Anarchist Alexander Berkman dies
description: Famous Lithuanian-born Jewish anarchist Alexander Berkman shot himself, unable to endure the pain of a longstanding illness. Initially paralysed by the bullet in his spine, he sank into a coma and died at 10pm.

title: Haymarket defendant Michael Schwab dies of prison TB
description: Michael Schwab, German-American labour organiser, Haymarket defendant, and anarchist, died from tuberculosis contracted whilst in prison. He was framed along with seven others for organising for the 8-hour day.

title: Grabow massacre of Louisiana timber workers
description: The Grabow massacre took place in Louisiana, when striking workers of the Brotherhood of Timber Workers, affiliated with the IWW, got into a shootout with hired thugs. In the end, 4 died and about 50 were wounded.

title: Hamburg massacre suppresses Black voters in SC
description: The Hamburg massacre began when an armed white mob of Democrats — the Red Shirts — marched on a majority Black and Republican district in Hamburg, South Carolina and killed six to suppress Black votes.

title: Goldman and Berkman sentenced to two years prison
description: Jewish anarchists Emma Goldman and Alexander Berkman were sentenced to two years prison, a $10,000 fine and deportation to Russia for violating conscription law during WWI.

title: Black anarchist Martin Sostre arrested in COINTELPRO frame-up
description: Black anarchist Martin Sostre was arrested at his bookstore in Buffalo, New York and charged with 'sale of narcotics, riot, arson, and assault.' The charges were fabricated as part of the COINTELPRO. He was sentenced to 41 years.

title: Eldridge and Kathleen Cleaver flee to Algiers
description: Black Panthers Kathleen and Eldridge Cleaver arrived in Algiers at the invitation of the Algerian government. Eldridge was wanted in the US after jumping bail following a clash with police in Oakland in 1968.

title: Black revolutionary Assata Shakur born
description: Black revolutionary Assata Shakur was born in Queens, NYC. She joined the Black Panthers and the Black Liberation Army urban guerrilla group until she was arrested and jailed. Escaping in 1979 she fled to Cuba where she still lives.

title: Frank Valdes beaten to death by Florida prison guards
description: Nine guards at the Florida State Prison in Starke entered the cell of Latino prisoner Frank Valdes armed with stun-guns, pepper spray and mace and beat and stomped him to death. The guards filed false reports and none were convicted.

title: Leonard Peltier escapes from Lompoc prison
description: Native American political prisoner Leonard Peltier escaped from Lompoc Federal Correctional Institution in California. Peltier was a leader in the American Indian Movement and was convicted in 1977 of killing two FBI agents.

title: Berkman tries to assassinate Henry Clay Frick
description: Jewish anarchist Alexander Berkman tried to assassinate the industrialist Henry Clay Frick, responsible for the murder of nine striking miners by Pinkerton thugs. Berkman shot him twice and stabbed him but Frick survived.

title: Chicago race riots begin after racist beach killing
description: A week of riots erupted in Chicago after a white man threw stones at a young African-American man on a racially segregated beach, killing him, and police refused to arrest the perpetrators. 1000s of Black homes were looted and burned.

title: Oklahoma State Penitentiary uprising crushed
description: Police and National Guard troops entered the Oklahoma State Penitentiary to quell the prisoners' uprising that had begun two days before and left four prisoners dead. The revolt destroyed 24 buildings and caused $30 million in damage.

title: Hilo massacre of striking Hawaiian dockers
description: The Hilo massacre took place in Hawaii, when police opened fire at an unarmed, multiracial group of striking dock workers, injuring 50. They were fighting for equal pay with dock workers on the US West Coast.

title: Yetta Stromberg arrested for displaying red flag
description: 19-year-old communist Yetta Stromberg was arrested with six others for having a red flag in California, one of 24 states which banned socialist/anarchist flags. She took her case to the Supreme Court and won, overturning the ban.

title: Marin County courthouse incident kills Jonathan Jackson
description: The Marin County courthouse incident took place when Jonathan Jackson attempted to free the three Soledad Brothers. A firefight broke out and four were killed, including Jackson. Ruchell Cinque Magee was later jailed for 53 years.

title: IWW general strike against Sacco-Vanzetti executions
description: The IWW union attempted a national general strike against the executions of anarchist workers Sacco and Vanzetti. It was particularly strong in Colorado coalmines, where the stoppage lasted for two days.

title: Watts riots erupt in Los Angeles
description: After an altercation between a Black family and Los Angeles police officers, the Watts neighbourhood exploded into six days of rioting with mass looting and anti-police violence in a turning point in the civil rights movement.

title: Anarchist Carlo Tresca arrested in the US
description: Italian-American anarchist Carlo Tresca was arrested in the US, supposedly the home of free speech, for writing articles denouncing fascism and the Italian monarchy.

title: Nat Turner's rebellion of enslaved people begins
description: Nat Turner's rebellion of enslaved Africans began in Southampton County, Virginia. Over the next two days, his band of rebels travelled to multiple plantations, freeing enslaved people and killing the enslavers until being repressed.

title: Camp Logan mutiny of Black soldiers in Houston
description: The Camp Logan mutiny took place when 156 African-American soldiers in segregated Houston, Texas clashed with police after officers assaulted and arrested two of them. 19 were later executed and 41 were given life sentences.

title: Gabriel's planned slave insurrection in Virginia
description: A group of enslaved people in Virginia, US, met to recruit others for an insurrection planned for 30 August, led by Gabriel, an enslaved 24-year-old. Unfortunately, the plot was betrayed and 26 of the participants were executed.

title: Battle of Blair Mountain begins in West Virginia
description: The Battle of Blair Mountain, the largest armed rebellion in the US since the civil war, began. 10,000 striking West Virginia coal miners battled with armed strikebreakers following the killing of miners, until disarmed by federal troops.

title: Shays' Rebellion of war veterans begins in Massachusetts
description: In the wake of the American revolution, war veterans and poor farmers shut down courts in Massachusetts and launched an armed uprising in protest at the debt burden on veterans and high taxes on farmers.

title: Joan Little kills sexually assaulting jail guard, escapes
description: Black inmate Joan Little killed a white prison guard in self-defence from sexual assault and escaped from the Beaufort County jail in South Carolina, US. She faced the death penalty, but with widespread support was acquitted.

title: Abbie Hoffman arrested in NYC drug sting
description: Anti-war and Yippie activist Abbie Hoffman was arrested with 3 others by undercover police in NYC for selling cocaine. Hoffman jumped bail and went underground, but eventually surrendered to authorities in 1980.

title: Ruben Salazar killed by police at Chicano protest
description: Ruben Salazar, US Army vet and the first Mexican-American mainstream media journalist to write about the Chicano community, was killed by police during an anti-Vietnam war protest. A 10-inch teargas projectile was shot at his head.

title: Fred Hampton born in Summit, Illinois
description: Leading Black Panther Fred Hampton was born in Summit, Illinois. He was key in forming links between the Panthers and working class people of all races in the Rainbow Coalition. At 21 he was assassinated by the FBI and Chicago police.

title: Puerto Rican Labour Day riot in Newark, NJ
description: Puerto Rican attendees at a Labour Day event in Newark, New Jersey rioted against police after years of brutality, as well as poor living and economic standards. The police department mobilized virtually its entire 1,500-member force.

title: Danziger Bridge police shootings in New Orleans
description: The Danziger Bridge shootings took place, when several New Orleans police officers opened fire with assault weapons on an unarmed African-American family. One man was shot five times in the back and a teenage girl was shot four times.

title: Civil rights activist Claudette Colvin born
description: African-American nurse and civil rights activist Claudette Colvin was born in Montgomery, AL. At 15, she was arrested and convicted for refusing to give up her seat on a bus for white passengers, several months before Rosa Parks.

title: Chiquola Mill massacre of striking SC textile workers
description: Sheriff's deputies and armed strikebreakers in South Carolina fired on striking textile workers at the Chiquola mill, killing seven and wounding at least 20. The massacre took place amidst a national strike of textile workers.

title: Attica prison uprising begins
description: The Attica prison uprising began when racial groups united and took control of the prison, demanding improvements. The governor sent in troopers who then killed 39, including eight guards whom they claimed were killed by prisoners.

title: Racists try to assassinate Fannie Lou Hamer
description: White supremacists attempted to assassinate Black civil rights activist Fannie Lou Hamer in Mississippi. While staying with her friend Mary Tucker, racists drove by, firing 16 shots at her, all of which missed.

title: ACT UP infiltrates the New York Stock Exchange
description: Seven members of the AIDS campaign group ACT UP infiltrated the New York Stock Exchange and chained themselves to a balcony in protest at the high cost of the only AIDS drug, AZT. Shortly after, Wellcome cut its price by over 20%.

title: Lincoln Brigade veterans hauled before SACB
description: Veterans of the Abraham Lincoln Brigade — US volunteers in the Spanish Civil War — were brought before the Subversive Activities Control Board as the government attempted to classify them as a subversive organisation.

title: Orlando Letelier assassinated by Pinochet's agents in DC
description: Chilean socialist refugee Orlando Letelier and think tank worker Ronni Moffitt were murdered in Washington, DC by a car bomb planted by US and UK-backed dictator General Pinochet's secret police.

title: Milwaukee 14 burn draft cards with homemade napalm
description: 14 men (including five priests and a minister) raided a federal office in Milwaukee, took 10,000 draft cards, and set them on fire with homemade napalm in protest of the Vietnam war.

title: Seattle passes gun law to disarm Black Panthers
description: Seattle city council brought in a gun-control law at the behest of the Republican mayor to prevent self-defence patrols by the Black Panthers. Many gun-control laws were introduced around then with NRA support to disarm African-Americans.

title: Elaine massacre of Black sharecroppers in Arkansas
description: The Elaine massacre began in Arkansas, US, when hundreds of African-Americans were murdered and tortured by white racists and security forces, after Black farm workers tried to organise for better pay.

title: Emma Goldman's incitement-to-riot trial begins in NYC
description: Jewish anarchist immigrant Emma Goldman began her trial in NYC. Found guilty of incitement to riot, she was sent to Manhattan's city prison and later sentenced to a year in Blackwell's Island Penitentiary.

title: Sam Melville bombs NYC Army Induction Center
description: The Army Induction Centre on Whitehall Street in New York City was bombed by Sam Melville and David Hughey. There were no injuries. It was the 2nd time the building had been bombed by anti-war activists, and was subsequently closed.

title: DC jail prisoners seize cellblock and take hostages
description: Approximately fifty inmates at the Washington D.C. jail seized control of a cellblock and held twelve jail officials hostage, demanding improved conditions and reductions in overcrowding.

title: Black Panther Party for Self-Defense founded
description: In Oakland, CA, the Black Panther Party for Self Defence was set up. The Party would exist, despite heavy repression, until 1982 and did things like feed breakfast to children and run community health clinics.

title: John Brown raids the Harpers Ferry armory
description: Abolitionists led by John Brown, including Black people recruited by Harriet Tubman, raided a federal armoury in Harpers Ferry, WV, to get weapons to arm enslaved people. Several were killed, and Brown later executed for treason.

title: Empire Zinc strike begins in New Mexico
description: Miners in New Mexico went on strike against the Empire Zinc Company for equal pay and treatment for whites and Mexicans. When strikers were banned from picketing, their wives took over and after 15 months they won.

title: Eugene V. Debs dies
description: US socialist and labour leader Eugene V. Debs died. He helped found both the American Railway Union and the Industrial Workers of the World union, and was jailed in 1919 for his opposition to World War I.

title: First deportation under Anarchist Exclusion Act
description: English anarchist-communist John Turner was arrested after his lecture in NYC. He was the first person deported from the US for violation of the 1903 Anarchist Exclusion Act. After prison, he made bail and was sent home to England.

title: Black Panther Huey Newton wounded and jailed in shootout
description: Black Panther Huey Newton was stopped by police. A shootout began leaving Newton and two cops wounded, one fatally. Newton was handcuffed to his hospital bed, beaten and later jailed but freed on appeal after a massive campaign.

title: Anarchist Leon Czolgosz executed for killing McKinley
description: Anarchist Leon Czolgosz was executed for the murder of US President McKinley.

title: Nat Turner captured in Virginia
description: Enslaved rebel leader Nat Turner was captured in Virginia. Two months prior, Turner and six other enslaved men launched a revolt, killing enslavers. He was tried, hanged and skinned. 100s of other Black people were murdered in revenge.

title: Federal troops break US miners' strike
description: 425,000 miners across the US went on strike for a 30 hour week and a 60% wage increase. The strike was banned by the government, who moved in federal troops, and the union caved, calling off the strike.

title: Jane Fonda arrested on bogus drug charges
description: Actor and Vietnam GI resistance supporter Jane Fonda was arrested at Cleveland airport after supporting Vietnam Veterans Against the War and charged with drug smuggling. The bogus charges were dismissed.

title: UFW union office bombed in California
description: A regional office of the United Farm Workers union in California was bombed. The UFW — largely made up of Latine and Filipine workers — had been on strike for weeks in a jurisdictional dispute with the Teamsters union.

title: Everett massacre of IWW members in Washington
description: At least five members of the revolutionary Industrial Workers of the World union were massacred by police and bosses' thugs in Everett, WA, after a ship carrying union members from Seattle was fired on.

title: First Palmer Raids target Russian Workers
description: A date specifically chosen due to it being the 2nd anniversary of the Russian revolution, FBI and police launched the 1st Palmer raids against the anarchist Union of Russian Workers in multiple US cities, arresting hundreds.

title: Wesley Everest lynched after Centralia incident
description: The Centralia incident took place, during which IWW member and war veteran Wesley Everest was lynched. A mob had attacked the union hall, and some were killed in self defence. Everest was murdered, and his killers went unpunished.

title: Helen Woodson and others damage Missouri missile silo
description: Peace activists damaged a nuclear missile silo in Missouri with a pneumatic drill. Four, including mother of 11 Helen Woodson, were arrested and jailed for very long sentences. Woodson ended up serving 27 years inside.

title: Jean Seberg born; later destroyed by COINTELPRO
description: American screen icon and Black Panther supporter Jean Seberg, who was hounded to death by the FBI, was born. She was targeted by COINTELPRO, which planted fake stories leading to the death of her premature baby and then her suicide.

title: Night of Terror against jailed US suffragists
description: The 'Night of Terror' began at the Occoquan Workhouse in Virginia, in which 33 suffragist prisoners were brutally tortured and beaten by guards after being arrested picketing the White House.

title: Oklahoma students walk out for Julius Jones
description: Over 1800 students across Oklahoma City walked out of class to protest the upcoming execution of Julius Jones, a Black man whom many believe was wrongfully convicted of murder. Jones' sentence was commuted during the protest.

title: IWW songwriter Joe Hill executed by firing squad
description: Joe Hill, Swedish-American IWW union member and songwriter, was executed by firing squad for a murder he did not commit. In his final letter he wrote: 'don't waste any time in mourning. Organize.'

title: Ricardo Flores Magón dies in Leavenworth Prison
description: Mexican anarchist communist of Zapotec descent, Ricardo Flores Magón, died after months of illness and neglect in Leavenworth Prison, Kansas. He was one of the major thinkers and activists of the Mexican revolution.

title: Thibodaux massacre of Black sugar workers
description: The Thibodaux massacre occurred when Louisiana militia shot and killed at least 50 unarmed Black sugar workers in the Knights of Labor striking for a wage in cash (not company scrip) and lynched two strike leaders.

title: Mississippi passes Black Code reintroducing slavery
description: Mississippi passed a Black Code to reintroduce slavery post-civil war. They prevented Black people from renting land, required them to work for whites and barred them from changing jobs. Violators could be jailed and enslaved legally.

title: Custer leads Washita massacre of Cheyenne
description: A detachment of US troops under the command of General Custer ignored orders to kill only warriors and massacred 103 sleeping Cheyenne in the so-called 'Battle of the Washita'.

title: US nuns murdered by US-trained Salvadoran death squads
description: Four US Catholic nuns were kidnapped, raped and murdered by government-backed and US-trained right-wing 'death squads' during the El Salvadorian civil war. The US later sheltered the killers.

title: Fred Hampton murdered in FBI-Chicago police raid
description: Chicago Black Panther leader Fred Hampton was murdered while asleep in his bed during a raid on his apartment by Chicago Police in conjunction with the FBI. Mark Clark was also killed, and other Panthers wounded.

title: First US gay rights group founded; arrests follow
description: The first gay rights organisation in the US, the Society for Human Rights, was founded in Chicago by postal worker and veteran Henry Gerber. It lasted a few months until police arrested Gerber and several others.

title: Black revolutionary Kuwasi Balagoon dies in prison
description: Black revolutionary Kuwasi Balagoon died in prison of an AIDS-related illness. One of the Panther 21 defendants, he later joined the Black Liberation Army and helped liberate Assata Shakur from prison.

title: Sitting Bull killed at Standing Rock Reservation
description: Hunkpapa Lakota leader Tȟatȟáŋka Íyotake (Sitting Bull) was killed by Indian police in the Standing Rock Reservation in South Dakota when he refused to cooperate with arrest.

title: Emma Goldman defies police to address IWW in Paterson
description: Jewish anarchist Emma Goldman addressed members of the IWW on anarchism in Paterson, NJ, despite police warnings barring her from speaking. Audience members ended up fighting police to release her.

title: Wounded Knee massacre of Lakota by US troops
description: The Wounded Knee massacre took place in South Dakota when US troops massacred over 200 Lakota men, women and children, many unarmed and fleeing.

title: Frank Steunenberg assassinated; Haywood charged
description: Former Idaho Gov. Frank Steunenberg was assassinated. Elected with workers votes, he later used federal troops to crush a strike. IWW Bill Haywood was charged but acquitted by jury of poor farmers, who actually thought he did it!

title: Marie Equi convicted of sedition for anti-war banner
description: Radical doctor, lesbian, IWW supporter and abortion provider Marie Equi was found guilty of sedition in the US for unfurling an anti-war banner. Sentenced to three years in San Quentin prison, she served one and a half.
